prompt:
A AlignSpace Offline Collaboration application for small cross-functional teams (design, product, marketing/ops) working in network-isolated offices to align quickly inside shared “rooms” that feel like everyone is gathered around the same desk. Users enter via a local profile screen using username + password stored on the device (a privacy gate for the shared computer, not a security boundary), and the UI returns to a lock screen after 20 minutes of inactivity to reduce shoulder-surfing risk. Inside each room, the main canvas is a shared whiteboard built on Canvas/SVG with pen, shapes, and image stickers; every element supports an optional note up to 20,000 characters for specs or decisions. A sticky-note wall supports drag-and-drop grouping and CSV bulk import (up to 1,000 rows per import, required columns: title and body; row-level errors are shown in a Modal with downloadable error CSV). Teams discuss work via threaded comments attached to elements and a lightweight chat Drawer; each chat message is limited to 500 characters, each local user is limited to 10 messages per minute, and each room retains the most recent 500 chat messages. Presence indicators (avatars with “active/idle” labels) appear in the room header and update across same-machine tabs, and an activity feed/changelog panel records key actions (create/edit/delete/move, snapshot/rollback, import/export) for quick traceability. A local “ops console” view (UI role switch) provides an announcement banner editor, featured template carousel configuration, a sensitive-word/keyword library used for on-screen warnings in chat/comments, and a rules/templates library for room starter kits; it also offers local event tracking and funnel-style analytics (e.g., room created → first whiteboard edit → first comment) plus canary toggles for experimental UI behaviors. Optional collaboration-adjacent tools include a meal/refreshment planner with an offline nutrient table to calculate macros/micros from search and barcode entry/scanning (camera-based scanning when available, otherwise manual entry), and a booking/order workflow for internal requests (e.g., reserving a review slot or ordering snacks) with a clear state machine (draft, pending, approved, paid-marked, canceled, refunding-marked, completed), configurable cancellation/reschedule policies, printable receipts, and CSV export.

The app is implemented with Vanilla HTML/CSS/JavaScript (LocalStorage) and runs fully offline with no backend services. Room data, whiteboard assets, images, chat/comment threads, the relationship graph (friend requests/accept/reject/withdraw, groups/notes, and a local blocklist), nutrient tables, orders, and up to 50 room snapshots are persisted in IndexedDB; lightweight preferences like UI role, last-opened room, notification toggles, and template defaults are stored in LocalStorage. The system auto-saves (“lands data”) every 5 seconds while the app is open, enforces a per-room storage cap of 200 MB, and shows a Toast warning at 180 MB with a guided cleanup view (largest images, oldest snapshots). Export/import uses the browser File API plus Blob-based downloads to create offline packages (single file ≤ 50 MB); imports merge by “last modified wins,” and if the same element is edited more than twice within 10 seconds during a merge window, the app generates a conflict duplicate and flags it in the activity feed. Multi-tab synchronization uses BroadcastChannel so edits, presence, notifications (inbox retains 200 items), and conflict warnings propagate instantly across tabs, while Web Workers handle heavy imports, snapshot creation, and report exports to keep the UI responsive; a Service Worker may be used to cache static assets for installable, always-offline use.
